name: openstaad-mcp
description: MCP server for Bentley STAAD.Pro via the OpenSTAAD API
long_description:
The OpenSTAAD MCP server enables Claude to interact with your STAAD.Pro models and perform various time-consuming tasks like load cases definition, data extraction, repetitive property setting and more.

## Key Features
- **Fast and flexible**: Enjoy minimal latency, interact with every STAAD.Pro features covered by the OpenSTAAD API.
- **AI-friendly**: Provides documentation, guidance and feedback via dedicated tools to help your AI agent ramp up quickly on the STAAD.Pro API.
- **Multi-instance support**: Connects to multiple running STAAD.Pro instances simultaneously to parallelize tasks across models.
- **Privacy-first**: All processing happens locally on your machine. No data is sent to the cloud. No telemetry.

This server requires having STAAD.Pro installed locally and running. If you don't already have it, download [STAAD.Pro (www.bentley.com)](https://www.bentley.com/software/staad) to get started.

Terms: Your use of this connector is subject to the [Bentley Systems Terms of Use (www.bentley.com)](https://www.bentley.com/legal/web-properties-terms-of-use).

tools.name: openstaad_discover_api
tools.description: Discover available API guidance and skills. Call this before using other openstaad-mcp tools to understand the API surface and see what skills are available. Then use ``openstaad_read_skills`` with one or more specific skill names to load full guidance.

tools.name: openstaad_read_skills
tools.description: Read one or more skills by name. Use ``openstaad_discover_api`` first to list available skills. Each skill provides domain-specific guidance (e.g. analysis, geometry, loads). Pass skill names like ``['staad-analysis']`` or sub-paths like ``['staad-steel-design/assets/DESIGN_CODES']`` to read reference files within a skill.

tools.name: openstaad_list_instances
tools.description: List all running STAAD.Pro instances. Returns alias (e.g. staadPro1), pid, file_path, and version for each. Pass the alias to openstaad_execute_code when multiple instances are running.

tools.name: openstaad_get_status
tools.description: Check the connection to a STAAD.Pro instance. Pass instance alias (from openstaad_list_instances) to target a specific instance. Returns connection state, STAAD version, model path, and whether the executor is currently busy (executor_busy). Always call this before openstaad_execute_code if a previous execution may still be running.

tools.name: openstaad_execute_code
tools.description: Execute Python code against the OpenSTAAD API. Imports are blocked; sandbox provides staad, json, math, progress. Timeout and mode are auto-detected from code keywords (120s sync, 600s async for loops, 3600s async for analysis/design). Override with timeout= for precise control (e.g. elements×0.005 for large loops at ~3ms/call). Override with mode='async' to force background execution — returns {job_id}, poll with openstaad_get_job_result.

tools.name: openstaad_get_job_result
tools.description: IMPORTANT: always display the returned `message` field to the user so they see progress. Long-polls a running job (server holds up to wait_seconds before responding) or returns the full result when done. Check `strategy`: 'poll' → call again with wait_seconds=next_wait_seconds; 'await_user_trigger' → stop polling, tell user job is still running, wait for their request.
